programme.title: Léon: The Professional
programme.desc: After her father, step-mother, step-sister and little brother are killed by her father's employers, the 12-year-old daughter of an abject drug dealer manages to take refuge in the apartment of a professional hitman who at her request teaches her the methods of his job so she can take her revenge on the corrupt DEA agent who ruined her life by killing her beloved brother.

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
programme.desc: After being snatched from the Green Place of Many Mothers, while the tyrants Dementus and Immortan Joe fight for power and control, the young Furiosa must survive many trials as she puts together the means to find her way home.

programme.title: Aliens
programme.desc: Ripley, the sole survivor of the Nostromo's deadly encounter with the monstrous Alien, returns to Earth after drifting through space in hypersleep for 57 years. Although her story is initially met with skepticism, she agrees to accompany a team of Colonial Marines back to LV-426.

programme.title: Ballerina
programme.desc: An assassin trained in the traditions of the Ruska Roma organization sets out to seek revenge after her father's death.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: After arriving in India, Indiana Jones is asked by a desperate village to find a mystical stone. He agrees – and stumbles upon a secret cult plotting a terrible plan in the catacombs of an ancient palace.

programme.title: The Chronicles of Riddick
programme.desc: The wanted criminal Richard Bruno Riddick arrives on a planet called Helion Prime and finds himself up against an invading empire called the Necromongers, an army that plans to convert or kill all humans in the universe.

programme.title: Nobody 2
programme.desc: Workaholic assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed vacation to the small tourist town of Plummerville. However, he soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a corrupt theme-park operator, a shady sheriff, and a bloodthirsty crime boss.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
programme.desc: There is a new criminal mastermind at large (Professor Moriarty) and not only is he Holmes’ intellectual equal, but his capacity for evil and lack of conscience may give him an advantage over the detective.

programme.title: Underworld
programme.desc: Vampires and werewolves have waged a nocturnal war against each other for centuries. But all bets are off when a female vampire warrior named Selene, who's famous for her strength and werewolf-hunting prowess, becomes smitten with a peace-loving male werewolf, Michael, who wants to end the war.

programme.title: Austin Powers in Goldmember
programme.desc: The world's most shagadelic spy continues his fight against Dr. Evil. This time, the diabolical doctor and his clone, Mini-Me, team up with a new foe—'70s kingpin Goldmember. While pursuing the team of villains to stop them from world domination, Austin gets help from his dad and an old girlfriend.

programme.title: Happy Gilmore
programme.desc: A Hockey player wannabe finds out that he has the most powerful golf drive in history. He joins the P.G.A. tour to make some money to save grandma's house. The downside is that his hockey player mentality doesn't really go on the P.G.A. tour. Especially with the favorite to win the championship.i

programme.title: The Toy
programme.desc: On one of his bratty son Eric's annual visits, the plutocrat U.S. Bates takes him to his department store and offers him anything in it as a gift. Eric chooses a black janitor who has made him laugh with his antics. At first the man suffers many indignities as Eric's "toy," but gradually teaches the lonely boy what it is like to have and to be a friend.

programme.title: Austin Powers: The Spy Who Shagged Me
programme.desc: When diabolical genius Dr. Evil travels back in time to steal superspy Austin Powers's ‘mojo,’ Austin must return to the swingin' '60s himself - with the help of American agent, Felicity Shagwell - to stop the dastardly plan. Once there, Austin faces off against Dr. Evil's army of minions to try to save the world in his own unbelievably groovy way.

programme.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail 1974
programme.desc: King Arthur, accompanied by his squire, recruits his Knights of the Round Table, including Sir Bedevere the Wise, Sir Lancelot the Brave, Sir Robin the Not-Quite-So-Brave-As-Sir-Lancelot and Sir Galahad the Pure. On the way, Arthur battles the Black Knight who, despite having had all his limbs chopped off, insists he can still fight. They reach Camelot, but Arthur decides not to enter, as "it is a silly place".

programme.title: Idiocracy
programme.desc: Corporal Joe Bauers, a decidedly average American, is selected for a top-secret hibernation program but is forgotten and left to awaken to a future so incredibly moronic that he's easily the most intelligent person alive.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: Home Alone
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Kevin McCallister makes the most of the situation after his family unwittingly leaves him behind when they go on Christmas vacation. When thieves try to break into his home, he puts up a fight like no other.

programme.title: Ferris Bueller's Day Off
programme.desc: A brash, cocky high school senior, tired of skipping school to spend a boring day at home, is determined to enjoy an epic day roaring around his favorite Chicago sites, enlisting his best friend and girlfriend to join him on the adventure.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Monty Python's Life of Brian
programme.desc: Brian Cohen is an average young Jewish man, but through a series of ridiculous events, he gains a reputation as the Messiah. When he's not dodging his followers or being scolded by his shrill mother, the hapless Brian has to contend with the pompous Pontius Pilate and acronym-obsessed members of a separatist movement. Rife with Monty Python's signature absurdity, the tale finds Brian's life paralleling Biblical lore, albeit with many more laughs.

programme.title: Spaceballs
programme.desc: When the nefarious Dark Helmet hatches a plan to snatch Princess Vespa and steal her planet's air, space-bum-for-hire Lone Starr and his clueless sidekick fly to the rescue. Along the way, they meet Yogurt, who puts Lone Starr wise to the power of "The Schwartz." Can he master it in time to save the day?

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop
programme.desc: Fast-talking, quick-thinking Detroit street cop Axel Foley has bent more than a few rules and regs in his time, but when his best friend is murdered, he heads to sunny Beverly Hills to work the case like only he can.

programme.title: Friday After Next
programme.desc: Craig and Day Day have finally moved out of their parents houses and into their own crib. The cousins work nights at a local mall as security guards. When their house is robbed on Christmas Eve they team up to track him down.

programme.title: The Naked Gun: From the Files of Police Squad!
programme.desc: A rerun of many of the gags from the television series Police Squad! (1982). An Airplane! (1980)-type spoof, this time with the an incompetent Lieutenant Frank Drebin, who always "gets his man". Visual gags come thick and fast, and it's impossible to catch them all with one viewing. The plot: Queen Elizabeth II of England is coming to town, and Vincent Ludwig has plans to assassinate her using a brainwashed baseball player.

programme.title: Police Academy 3: Back in Training
programme.desc: After it's announced that one of the two police academies in the state will be closed down, Lassard's alumni return to the school to train new recruits and compete against the other academy.

programme.title: Frankenstein
programme.desc: The 1931 horror movie "Frankenstein", tells the tale of when Henry Frankenstein who is a doctor, trying to discover a way to make a living human using human body parts from the recently deceased. He succeeds, but his creation is looked upon as a monster by the local inhabitants, who wish to destroy it.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Spacemonster
programme.desc: When an atomic war on Mars destroys the planet's women, it's up to Martian Princess Marcuzan and her right-hand man Dr. Nadir to travel to earth and kidnap women for new breeding stock. Landing in Puerto Rico, they shoot down a NASA space capsule manned by an android. With his electronic brain damaged, the android terrorizes the island while the Martians raid beaches and pool parties.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Wolf Man
programme.desc: The resurrected Wolf Man, seeking a cure for his malady, enlists the aid of a mad scientist, who claims he will not only rid the Wolf Man of his nocturnal metamorphosis, but also revive the frozen body of Frankenstein's inhuman creation.

programme.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: After a terrified toy salesman is mysteriously attacked and brought to the hospital clutching the year's most popular Halloween mask, Dr. Daniel Challis sets out to investigate the strange events and finds himself thrust into a nightmarish conspiracy.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: House of Dracula
programme.desc: A scientist working on cures for rare afflictions, such as a bone softening agent made from molds to allow him to correct the spinal deformity of his nurse, finds the physical causes of lycanthropy in wolf-man Larry Talbot and of vampirism in Count Dracula, but himself becomes afflicted with homicidal madness while exchanging blood with Dracula.

programme.title: House of Frankenstein
programme.desc: After escaping from prison, the mad Dr. Gustav Niemann and his loyal hunchbacked assistant, Daniel, revive not only Count Dracula, but the Wolf Man and Frankenstein's Monster as well, all in order to exact revenge upon Niemann's many enemies.

programme.title: House of Wax
programme.desc: A sculptor opens a wax museum to showcase the likenesses of famous historical figures, but quickly runs into trouble when his business partner demands the exhibits become more extreme in order to increase profits.

programme.title: I Bury the Living
programme.desc: Through a series of macabre "coincidences," the newly-elected director of a cemetery (Richard Boone) begins to believe that he can cause the deaths of living owners of burial plots by merely changing the push-pin color from white (living) to black (dead) on a large wall map of the cemetery that notes those plots.

programme.title: Invaders from Mars
programme.desc: A young boy learns that space aliens are taking over the minds of earthlings.

programme.title: Invisible Agent
programme.desc: The Invisible Man's grandson uses his secret formula to spy on Nazi Germany.

programme.title: Island of Terror
programme.desc: A small, isolated island community's terrorised from a horde of creeping, blobbish, tentacled monsters which liquefy and digest the bones from living creatures. The community struggles to fight back, against these horrors.

programme.title: Killer Klowns from Outer Space
programme.desc: When a small town is invaded by aliens from outer space who are capturing and killing the townspeople, no one takes them seriously. Why? The aliens all look like circus clowns, use weapons that look clown like, and all have painted on smiles. Only a few of the young people in the town realize the danger and of course no one believes them. Armed with an ice cream truck they try and rescue their friends.

programme.title: Killers from Space
programme.desc: Atomic scientist/pilot Doug Martin is missing after his plane crashes on an reconnaissance mission after a nuclear test. Miraculously appearing unhurt at the base later, he is given sodium amethol, but authorities are skeptical of his story that he was captured by aliens determined to conquer the Earth with giant monsters and insects. Martin vows to use existing technology to destroy them.

programme.title: Lady Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Frankenstein's daughter and his assistant/her lover continue his experiments in an attempt to rebuild his legacy after he is killed by his psychotic, murderous first monster.

programme.title: Night of the Living Dead
programme.desc: A ragtag group of Pennsylvanians barricade themselves in an old farmhouse to remain safe from a horde of flesh-eating ghouls that are ravaging the Northeast of the United States.

programme.title: Renfield
programme.desc: Renfield, Dracula's tortured henchman, is forced to capture prey for his master and do his every bidding. But now, after centuries of servitude, Renfield is ready to see if there is a life outside his boss's shadow.

programme.title: Son of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Returning to the ancestral castle long after the death of the monster, the son of Dr. Frankenstein meets a mad shepherd who is hiding the comatose creature. To clear the family name, he revives the creature and tries to rehabilitate him.

programme.title: The Best of Ringo Starr and his All Star Band so far
Buck Cherry and Some Cheryl Crow Woodstock 99
Tom Petty and The Heartbreakers - Storytellers (1999)
Led Zeppelin Live Madison Square Garden
The Rolling Stones Live From Rio
The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
G Love and Special Sauce - Woodstock 99 Act 03
NWA AWA Nite of Champions II
Svengoolie
programme.desc: A delicious, mysterious goo that oozes from the earth is marketed as the newest dessert sensation, but the tasty treat rots more than teeth when zombie-like snackers who only want to consume more of the strange substance at any cost begin infesting the world.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The power company cuts off the Conners' power. Dan and Roseanne must look at Darlene in a new light after David spends the night in her room.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Jackie are stunned when their mother announces she is moving to Lanford; Jackie agonizes over dating Fisher, a perfect, but much, much younger man.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne mortifies Darlene with a sweet sixteen party, then sends her off to her friends while the adults enjoy the festivities, which include a game of Truth or Dare; the Tildens move in next door; Darlene dyes her hair black to make Roseanne mad, but is shocked when Roseanne reacts differently.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: Looking for Loans in All the Wrong Places
programme.desc: Denied a loan to open the restaurant, Roseanne must turn to her mother; Darlene catches D.J. peeping at neighbor Molly.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne sends everyone to a party while she stays home -- and is visited by the ghosts of Halloween past, present, and future.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Nancy takes Roseanne and Jackie by surprise with the announcement that she and Marla are gay lovers; Bev announces she is moving into a Lanford retirement center, which leaves Roseanne feeling over the hill.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The Lunch Box welcomes its first customers, including a weirdo whose presence sends the gals to a self-defense class. Meanwhile, Arnie questions his own manhood after hearing that Nancy is gay.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne reluctantly allows Darlene to go to a rock concert in nearby Rockford with Molly -- who abandons her.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The staff have kittens when Mrs. Slocombe announces she's expecting a happy event, but it turns out that it's merely her cat that's pregnant; when she refuses to leave Tiddles unattended, she braves the ire of Captain Peacock by smuggling the cat into the ladies' fitting room.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.sub-title: A Change Is As Good As a Rest
programme.desc: The staff have fun temporarily working in Grace Brothers' toy department. Mr. Humphries enjoys a giant dollhouse, Mrs. Slocombe sells peeing dollies and Mr. Grainger gets sentimental playing with a train set.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc: Bill and Toony are forced to deal with all types of issues when the studio furnace unexpectedly breaks down.

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: Riley Greenleaf is a publisher and Alan Mallory, a successful writer, is his principal asset. But Alan Mallory has decided to change publishers. Riley threatens to kill Alan. Then he hires Eddie Kane, a Vietnam veteran, to murder Alan and to leave evidence that the killer is Riley himself, who has an excellent alibi. But a key will betray him.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Mrs. Naugatuck's strange behavior and wedding announcement surprise everyone.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: When Walter's business is in $12,000 debt and is facing bankruptcy, Maude suggests that she mortgage the house to get the funds, but a stubborn Walter won't let her, so she does anyway and ask Arthur to say the money came from him.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy's son, Michael, shows up on her doorstep with news that he's separated with his wife and needs a place to stay. However, Dorothy quickly gets tired of his freeloading and dumps him on Stan. Meanwhile, Rose's stress is getting terrible and is reluctant to talk to her boss about cutting back on her "things-to-do" list.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Blanche is shattered after her father, Big Daddy, passes away. However, when she goes back to her childhood home, she gets into a big argument with her sister, Virginia and decides not to attend the funeral.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.sub-title: Have Yourself A Very Little Christmas
programme.desc: It's the Christmas season and the girls are fed up with Christmas shopping in a heat wave. So they decide to buy gifts for the one person they pick from a hat. Later, the girls decide to serve Christmas dinner for the homeless, including a recently homeless Stan.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Blanche and Rose are concerned that Roland overworks himself and decide to compete to find him a new girlfriend. Rose finds a beautiful young girl and Blanche tracks down Roland's southern ex-girlfriend Tricia. Only when Tricia is already on the plane to Miami does Blanche find out that Roland hates her. Sophia is jealous of Chuy because he was chosen over her to represent the hotel at a television show.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Grady has been left in charge while Fred is visiting family in St. Louis and has promised to look after Lamont who comes home with a bloody nose, courtesy of Jo-Jo Jackson, after a fight over a woman. After taking a couple of karate lessons, Lamont goes back to the bar to confront Jo-Jo. However, a concerned Grady, decides tries to intervene.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: Will the Real Fred Sanford Please Do Something?
programme.desc: A woman, whom Fred once had a drunken fling with, stops by and becomes convinced that Grady is Fred. Grady is in shock and Lamont is no help trying to convince her otherwise. But Grady has an even bigger problem: The woman decides to hold him to the promise Fred made - marriage.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.sub-title: Oh, My Aching Back (aka Archie's Aching Back)
programme.desc: Archie is involved in a minor, non-injury car accident, then finds out that Lionel Jefferson's family used an insurance settlement to open a dry-cleaning business. He gets an idea ... and a sudden aching back.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie refuses to donate blood because he's afraid that his vital fluids might get mixed in with those of a different race.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Ellen & Stacy vs. Mindy & Robert vs. Sandi & Chris

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Cheryl & Saundra vs. Toni & Michael vs. Ann & Sherri
programme.desc: Ann & Sherri would later appear in the Sweep of Champions

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are frontier guides leading a minstrel show west. When hostile Indians run the horses run off they are stranded. They must contend with a snow storm and a marauding bear as well the Indians. After almost killing each other ice fishing they solve their problems by rigging up a sail on the wagon and sailing west. A wagon train full of show biz girls tours the West, and the Stooges are assigned to protect them from Indians. First appearance by Stooge player Dorothy Appleby (former Miss Maine).

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are janitors working in the offices of Doctors Harts, Burns and Belcher. Some crooks arrive seeking medical attention after their boss has been wounded in a shoot out with the cops. Mistaken for doctors, the boys are forced to operate on the wounded crook, but instead they accidentally dump him out the window into a passing police car. The rest of the gang chases them into a store room filled with dummies where the cops finally catch the bad guys.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooge's friend Claude sells them his old lemon of a car so they can take Shemp, who is sick with a toothache, camping. The car won't work and the boys are apparently out a bundle, when a car collector happens on the scene and offers to buy it at a premium. Claude backs out on the deal and gives the stooges their money back, only to discover the "collector" is an escaped lunatic.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are tree surgeons who are enlisted by a rich old man to find a mate for his rare puckerless persimmon tree. The boys sail to the tropical island of Rhum-Boogie to find the tree. When they arrive they are captured by the natives and will be eaten unless Curly marries the Chief's ugly daughter. The stooges escape with the tree and, after a confrontation with an alligator, sail off with their prize.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are engaged to the three daughters of a prison warden. When they learn that some crooks have taken over the prison and their prospective father-in-law has been locked up, they decide to go undercover to rescue him. The stooges sneak into the prison where they find a casino with a fancy party in progress. After swiping some formal attire, they crash the party and get candid camera evidence to expose the crooked goings-on. With the crooks behind bars once again, the stooges are able to get married and all ends well

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges make a whole batch of homemade beer, but get tossed in jail when Curly sells some to a cop. Their minor indiscretion turns into a forty year sentence when a keg of beer Curly has hidden under his coat explodes while the boys are being photographed. In prison the stooges get into more trouble with the warden and wind on the rockpile when they try to escape. Released as old men with long gray beards, the first thing Curly wants is a bottle of beer.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are private detectives hired to protect a rich politician. After the man disappears, the boys wander around his spooky mansion confronting various villains and a dangerous dame. The stooges vanquish the crooks (Shemp uses his "trusty shovel") and find the missing man.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer gropes for a gummy candy stuck to the babysitter's pants, she mistakes his intentions and turns the town against him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer prevents a nuclear meltdown,purely by picking a button to press at random, but he is declared a hero and asked to give a talk on plant safety. During the talk he is called upon to prevent another meltdown and exposed as a lucky chancer who only averted an accident by mistake. Marge goes to see Millhouse's mother who has barred her son from seeing Bart,whom she regards as a bad influence,and persuades her to change her mind.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer gets a bucket stuck on his head, and when it is removed the town is convinced that Bart has the powers of a faith healer. Then mobster Fat Tony tries to turn the boy's spiritual abilities into more sinister directions.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: As dissension continues to build within The Corporation, Paul Wight and WWE Champion The Rock put aside their differences to take on "Stone Cold" Steve Austin & Mankind. The Undertaker takes his campaign of evil right to Mr. McMahon's front door. Plus, Jim Ross takes up a familiar position, the New Age Outlaws go after championship gold, and more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: David’s photographer ex-boyfriend arrives in town to do a photo series on Moira. Johnny and Roland suspect that Bob is cheating at poker.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny tries to avoid the spread of lice in the motel. Moira tries to get out of having a flower garden named after her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max spend Caroline's first broke holiday season working at a department store's Santaland to earn extra money.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to find Chestnut a job in order to get him lodging for the winter. Meanwhile, Peach, the mother of the twins Max babysits, auditions for "Real Housewives of TriBeCa".

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max's stove breaks, Caroline tries to return some expensive rings to Barney's department store, but discovers her surname is no longer an asset at her former home away from home.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline discovers the secret ingredient to Max's cupcakes whilst in the midst of her new obsession couponing.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's new neighbor draws their suspicion.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to prove their cleaning skills to Sophie so she will hire them for her maid service.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Sophie's (Jennifer Coolidge) beauty overwhelms Earl, Caroline and Max wind up spending Valentine's Day at the hospital; Caroline tries to rekindle an old flame.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline have to make kosher cupcakes when they get a gig at a bar mitzvah.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max runs into an old boyfriend, when she accompanies Caroline to see her father in jail.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max spend their spring break dog-sitting at a couple's lavish apartment.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max convinces Caroline to partake in a clinical drug trial in an effort to raise money to help Caroline’s father.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After promising Earl to drop his tax return in the mail, Caroline learns that Max has never filed, and gets sidetracked trying to help her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The stakes are high when a socialite Manhattan mother hires Max and Caroline to serve cupcakes at her daughter's first birthday party.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And Martha Stewart Have a Ball (1)
programme.desc: Max and Caroline plan to attend a big fashion gala at the Metropolitan Museum of Art where Martha Stewart is scheduled to be a guest, in an attempt to have her taste their cupcakes and get her seal of approval.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to keep faith that Martha Stewart, who tasted and liked one of their cupcakes in last season's finale, will call and help support their business. Meanwhile, Caroline loses a lucky keepsake.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: With their season in a tailspin, Richmond try to right the ship against mighty Manchester City. Off the pitch, everyone faces their own setbacks.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: A friendly match takes the team to Amsterdam, where one night out unlocks truths for many.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Paulie takes pains to integrate his mother Nucci into the social world at Green Grove. Meanwhile, Furio faces up to his inner feelings during a trip to Italy to see his ailing father; Ralph introduces Tony to his new 'goomar,' Valentina; and Carmela finds a new source of investment income.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: After an aggressive team of reporters lowers the boom on Uncle Junior, his lawyers try a new strategy. Ralph has some fun at the expense of Paulie and his mother, but soon winds up with his own family problems. Meanwhile, Tony again finds refuge at the stables.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor has a hard time remaining hidden. Michael asks Tahani to assist a neighbor in finding her true purpose.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor's eagerness to learn ethics from Chidi becomes the full-time job he never wanted. Meanwhile, Michael and Janet focus their attention on a Neighborhood emergency.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Rhaenyra continues her seemingly futile search for a suitable match, Daemon returns Westeros to Kings Landing and stirs more trouble for the King. Rhaenyra learns a valuable lesson about desires and what it means to be a daughter of a King.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Daenerys offers a choice. Arya grows suspicious. Tyrion answers a good question.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: After a wild night out, a teenage girl is brought to Princeton Plainsborough with severely swollen appendages. The team must work to diagnose the young girl, who is less than honest about what happened the night she fell ill. As her condition worsens, she becomes unable to distinguish fact from fiction. Meanwhile, Cuddy, Wilson and House spend a weekend away from the hospital to attend a medical conference, but things don’t go as planned when House’s private investigator, Lucas returns.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: After House’s medical license is reinstated, he reclaims his role as Head of Diagnostics in time to treat Hank Hardwick, an adult film star admitted to Princeton Plainsborough for pulsating eye pain. Meanwhile, Cuddy is reminded that Princeton Plainsborough is not conducive to healthy personal relationships.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: While faced with community service for abusing an endangered insect, Homer begins assisting an elderly woman named Mrs. Bellamy. One night, Mrs. Bellamy is murdered, and Homer and Marge are accused of committing the crime.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A social worker is assigned to make the Simpson family functional after they get arrested for fighting while playing Monopoly. He helps them learn how to work together and function as a family. The moment is ruined when, Ginger and Amber, the barmaids who married Homer and Flanders while they were drunk in "Viva Ned Flanders", arrive at the Simpsons' home, which outrages Marge.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Strong Arms of the Ma
programme.desc: Marge gets mugged and becomes so traumatized that she develops Agoraphobia. She hides in the basement. Having little else to do, she decides to exercise with a weight set Homer bought from Rainier Wolfcastle's garage sale. She becomes so strong and confident that she is able to leave the basement. She continues working out and decides to start taking steroids so she can enter a bodybuilding competition. One night at Moe's Tavern she loses her temper and starts destroying the bar and fighting all of the patrons. Homer confronts Marge and says he misses the sweet woman that he married. Marge, horrified at what she has done, apologizes and decides to stop working out.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge discover that Apu is having an affair with the Squishee delivery lady at the Kwik-E-Mart. They decide to keep Apu's wife Manjula from finding out about it. However, she eventually learns of Apu's affair by watching store security tapes. She throws Apu out of the house and decides to file for divorce, but soon realizes that she misses him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: It's a Mad, Mad, Mad, Mad Marge
programme.desc: Otto's girlfriend seems to be usurping Marge's role when she moves in with the family after a dispute over heavy metal.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer builds a battle robot to win Bart's respect, and places it in a tournament against other robots. What Bart does not know, though, is that the robot is actually Homer in costume. Meanwhile, Snowball II dies and Lisa tries to find a replacement cat.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Guess Who's Coming to Criticize Dinner?
programme.desc: With Lisa's help, Homer uses his vast knowledge of food to become the town's leading restaurant critic. But when he gets petty and vindictive in his criticisms, all the other chefs in town try to kill him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa struggles with solving a simple puzzle and fears that the "Simpson gene” is suddenly wiping out her intelligence. Homer stages a family reunion in hopes of introducing Lisa to some Simpsons she can respect.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is hired to work in a Mafia bar run by Fat Tony (making his first appearance). Bart is late for work due to having been put in detention by Principal Skinner. Fat Tony announces that he and his men will "meet and greet" Skinner, and shortly after that, Skinner disappears. Both Bart and Fat Tony are charged with Skinner's murder, and at the trial, Fat Tony tries to pin the murder (and all of his other illegal activities) on Bart.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer prevents a nuclear meltdown,purely by picking a button to press at random, but he is declared a hero and asked to give a talk on plant safety. During the talk he is called upon to prevent another meltdown and exposed as a lucky chancer who only averted an accident by mistake. Marge goes to see Millhouse's mother who has barred her son from seeing Bart,whom she regards as a bad influence,and persuades her to change her mind.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty the Clown has dinner with the Simpsons, and reveals that he is Jewish, his real name is Herschel Krustofsky, and he is estranged from his rabbi father. They parted ways over Krusty's becoming a clown instead of following his father and becoming a rabbi. Bart and Lisa see Krusty becoming depressed, and they take it upon themselves to reunite him with his father.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al and Kelly pretend to be sick so they can avoid going with Peggy and Bud to Wanker County, but Kelly ends up really getting sick and Al has to care for her for the entire week which interrupts Al's fantasies of being with two beautiful women.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Bud's new driver's license portends bumper cars on the roadway. When Bud t-bones a Mercedes, Al sees an opportunity to clean up in court. At least he has hope. Losing his lawsuit hurt but what he goes through to payoff the settlement is truly painful.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al's softball team wants to bench him before the big championship, but Peggy, Kelly and Bud hold three of the required votes to omit him. Peggy is swayed by the hunk who will replace Al, while it takes food to get the kids' votes.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al doesn't mind Peggy going out with Marcy every night to a ballroom dance hall and dance the night away with a handsome man named Andy, until another man, named Pete, tells Al that Peggy is stealing someone's husband - his.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Now an aspiring model, Kelly invents a maneuver called "The Bundy Bounce" for her first audition as spokesperson for the new Allente car. But Bud's big mouth gives the idea to another model, so Kelly must take things into her own hands and remove the competition. Meanwhile, Peggy goes on a strike with housework because Al think's she's useless.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After Al returns home from work with a bump on the head, he sees (or thinks he sees) six little green aliens coming down to Earth and stealing his smelly socks to use as fuel for their spaceships. The only problem is no one sees them but Al, so everyone think's he's crazy when he tells others that he saw them.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al is under pressure from one too many busy days at the shoe store and the doctors have ordered him to relax. To combat stress, Al starts a vegetable garden. The combat turns physical when a rabbit starts sealing carrots from Al's garden and it becomes a war of wills: Bundy against Bunny.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: The Sanford home has become a campaign headquarters for the candidate Bob Williams and Fred wants nothing to do with it. However, once Fred discovers, Della Reese is working on the campaign of Williams' opponent, he goes all out for it even thought he's not for the candidate.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Donna receives a marriage proposal from another man and she decides to base her answer on whether or not Fred is ever going to ask her. Fred must make the choice now as to whether or not he is going to marry Donna. This leads Fred into turmoil and leads him to ask Elizabeth a heart felt question on whether or not she would object to him remarrying.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont is a judge for the Miss Watts Business Wife and the winner of the pageant wins $1,000. Fred quickly makes it clear that he will do anything to get his hands on the money. He even agrees to transform Aunt Esther into an elegant beauty as part of a bet he has with her husband, Woody.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont's friend, Ah Chew cooks dinner for the Sanfords one evening and a wary Fred is quite impressed at how good the food is. This leads Fred and Ah Chew to start a takeout business together with the headquarters in the Sanford home. Fred soon realizes that they should expand the business and turn the house into a Japanese restaurant.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris loses his wallet in the big city, and the big city give him the key to the city and other perks to make him feel better.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: When Chris was 12 he got a job as a paperboy so he could buy a submarine from the back of a comic book. Four to six hundred weeks later when the kit arrives, Chris and his father put it together; however when they embark on the sub's maiden voyage (in Chris's bathtub) they become trapped.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Parody of famous gangster pictures.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Spot-gag spoof of the Believe It, or Not! franchise.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky falls asleep after refusing to say the Pledge of Allegiance. Uncle Sam comes to him in his dreams and explains to him what the pledge means, and how it honors those who gave their life for the nation. Porky sees the error of his ways.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Parody of The Shooting of Dan McGrew.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Travelogue parody of a look all-round America.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Travelogue parody of a cruise ship travelling to Alaska.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky the police officer must investigate a haunted house.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: Beavis and Butt-head vs. The Vending Machine
programme.desc:
Beavis and Butt-head try various means to get their bag of snacks unstuck and out of the vending machine before they starve. videos:
Sweaty Nipples - "Demon Juice"
Elton John & RuPaul - "Don't Go Breakin' My Heart"
Seaweed - "Kid Candy"
Motley Crue - "Houligan's Holiday"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys drive Principal McVicker insane, sending him to a mental home.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The ""Positive Acting Teens"" group, led by Mr. Graham, seeks to rehabilitate some kids in detention with community service. Fat chance, since those kids happen to be Beavis and Butt-head.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
After playing "Iron Justice," Beavis has a near death experience, and dreams of meeting St. Peter, and Butt-head trying to bury him. videos:
Gary Numan - "Cars" Frank Black - "Los Angeles"
Tom Waits - "I Don't Want To Grow Up"

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head get trapped in a cardboard box while attempting to hide out in the school.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Needing money to replace the batteries in their TV remote control, Beavis and Butt-head offer to wash a neighbor's vintage car. They take it for a drive to air dry it, and get into an accident with Mr. Anderson.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Over the course of a few hours, the boys attempt to master the subtle techniques of the game...in broad daylight.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Hilarity ensues as some major intestinal cramps convince Beavis that he might be pregnant.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head try to solve an Escape Room only to wind up trapped in the bathroom.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Tantalized by a sexy yogurt advertisement on television, Beavis and Butt-Head decide they have to try some as soon as possible. Whey they get to the yogurt store they find the yogurt leaves a lot to be desired and the staff is less than sympathetic to their problems with it.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Randy's got the whole Playhouse barking at the word "go" - today's secret word. However, Pee-wee has bigger concerns. Jambi is sick, so Pee-wee has to call a genie-ologist! It's Dr. Jinga-Janga to the rescue, but he's got a wacky bedside manner that may just be worse than Jambi's disease - Mecca Lecca Hi Mecca Heinyitis!

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: While her house is being painted, Miss Yvonne accepts an invitation to stay at the Playhouse. Perfumey as she is, Miss Yvonne is a stinker of a houseguest! Since Pee-wee's too "nice" to ask her to leave, he has to get a little help from Jambi to get her house painted lickety split. Now, the Cowntess wants to stay over while her barn is painted. What will Pee-wee do?

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Oleg and Sophie’s plan to have Max and Caroline as Barbara’s godparents is derailed when Oleg’s domineering mother, Olga, arrives from the Ukraine for the baby’s baptism and fires Max and Caroline from their godparent duties.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline and Max are invited to speak about their business at Caroline’s alma mater, the University of Pennsylvania, Max takes the opportunity to give Caroline the college partying experience she never had. Also, Oleg’s attempts to bond with baby Barbara are rebuffed by a possessive Sophie, and Han practices his magic tricks with a reluctant Earl as his assistant.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Earl becomes concerned he may not be able to keep up when his long lost sweetheart, Pilar, visits from Cuba, and wants him to take her sightseeing around the country. Also, Max must decide whether it’s time to take her texting relationship with Randy to the next level.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Survivor Series teams get ready as WWE’s “Thanksgiving Night Tradition” is just days away. The Hulkamaniacs join “The Brother Love Show”. “Ravishing” Rick Rude, Honky Tonk Man and others are in action.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: When Dottie Minerva comes down with a bad case of mono, Tina gets her shot as soloist of the Hormone-iums. But as she rehearses for the big assembly, Tina realizes that her role in the play could ruin her social status. Meanwhile, Linda strikes gold with a business plan that she thinks could make the Belchers rich.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After Debra reads a newspaper article about people who enjoy being at work more than being at home, she convinces Ray to set up a home office.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Sick of reading Ally the same stories night after night, Debra decides to try her hand at writing a children's book.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: At a wedding, Doug takes a naughty picture with a disposable camera supplied by the bride and groom. After letting Danny take the blame, and after confessing to a priest, he has the dreaded task of coming forward with the truth.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie wins 4 company seats to Knicks games, but insists on taking a couple over Doug's friends. But when they realize Carrie's favored couple doesn't work for Doug, and Doug's favored couple doesn't work for Carrie, they decide on playing cupid. Meanwhile, Arthur digs himself a hole by pretending he can "fix" parking tickets.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry dates his maid, and blurs the fine line between professional relationship and prostitution. Kramer begins a long-distance relationship. Elaine becomes annoyed at all the faxes she's getting from Kramer's restaurant service and has her phone number changed. But she gets a new area code and that only complicates things. George gets an unwanted nickname at Kruger.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Gridlocked by a parade, Jerry verbally spars with another driver; George goes to a movie; and Kramer looks for a bathroom. Elaine, meanwhile, searches for a crossing point with a group of strangers.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan open up shop at Lanford Custom Cycle, but selling a bike proves an exhausting challenge, as does keeping the household fine-tuned with Becky in charge.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Jackie awakens and is horrified to find that she has slept with Arnie; Dan invites Leon home for the weekly poker game; D.J. trades his comics for a dog.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: When a purse saleswoman enters the office to sell her purses, she gets declined, but when Michael lays his eyes upon her, he decides she can stay at the office and sell her purses to the workers there. But not only Michael has his eyes on her, other employees and Jim look to share a date with her too.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: It's time for the "Dundies", Dunder Mifflin's annual awards banquet (held at the neighborhood Chili's), and the staff must endure the excruciating performance of their inept emcee, boss Michael Scott.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Just as corporate tells Toby, one of the workers at the Scranton branch, to give a seminar on sexual harassment issues, Michael receives a visit from his obnoxious sales-rep friend Todd Packer. The two chauvinistic friends try to prove that the office is not in need of harassment counseling, but only wind up making the situation worse, causing yet another zany day at the office.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons go to a market sale and receive a severed monkey's paw which can grant five wishes. Bart has magic powers so he can turn people into anything he wants if they be naughty to him and he turns Homer into a Jack-in-the-box. Mr Burns' puts Homer's brain into a robot.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer loses much of Lisa's respect for him after he botches the task of getting her a saxophone reed she needs for a talent show. Homer realizes that he's been ignoring Lisa since she was a baby and thinks that's what's causing problems now, so he buys Lisa a pony to change her opinion of him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Trying to be a better father, Homer helps Bart build a soapbox racer. Bart's racer is a flop, but Martin Prince's is the fastest and wins the race. When Martin is hurt, he asks Bart to fill in as driver so that at least one of them can beat Nelson Muntz in the race.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Partridge Family Get Shot will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross' nephew visits the show, but doesn't get along very well with the cast.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The A-Team Makes One Cup of Coffee Last 5 Hours will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The kids hang out - at the arcade, the library, malls and summer camp, after school, at the Burgery, around the set, outside prison, stores, locker and dressing rooms. They also hang laundry, hang tough, and generally just hang in there.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Wizard Shows You How to Make Rain will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Alasdair makes a weather machine, but it doesn't work correctly.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Knight Rider Wants a Ferrari will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast is jealous of Christine after she gets a raise.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Dunking for Dollars will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The series is sold to various foreign countries. Adam acts as millionaire Ronald Rump.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Love Connection Short Circuits will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Doug pretends to be Cupid and tries to fix up the entire cast with a girl/boyfriend.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. T Thinks He's a Girl will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Alasdair tries out different jobs to see which one is right for him. Lisa does a paper for school on what people want to do for their careers.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Super Monster Theatre will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
As everyone confesses what he or she is afraid of, the Locker Monster attacks Vanessa.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Family Ties Tied Much Too Tight will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross convinces Alasdair that he may be the descendant of a millionaire. Ross then sells fake family trees to the rest of the cast, showing them as being related to Napoleon.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Wreck of the Love Boat will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Lisa thinks that being optimistic will save her plant from dying.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Rambo Shoots the Ayatollah will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross decides to get rich by selling Instant Revenge to the cast members. Alasdair decides to do it in cognito as the Masked Revenger.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
This episode features the exciting battle of the Airline Travel Industry against the Circus Workers. Vic discusses Kenny's options for joining the circus. Guy Le Douche's cousin Geek substitutes in for him so he can attend his annual parole hearing.
Games include: Slippery Slope of Slanted Death; Foul Balls; Little Man in the Boat; Corn Holders; Mudballs

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The fit and muscular physical fitness industry challenges the burned-out musical team in this MXC battle. Also, Kenny shows us some new game prototypes.
Games include: Turtle Gut Check; Mounting the Spike; Clear Sphere of Fear; Jerk and Release

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The fashion people take on the bible-thumpers on this edition of MXC. Also, Vic and Kenny continue their tribute to the new First Network for Men.
Games include: Yankers Away; The Poisonous Horny Toad; Shroom Zoom; Runaway Stump

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The adult entertainment industry challenges the home improvement industry in this edition. Captain Tenneal gets a special visit from porn star Ivana Swallow.
Games include: Rotating Surfboard of Death; Dash to Death; Dope on a Rope; Elimination Idol

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Taking their chances in this episode, the gambling industry takes on the medical professionals. Also, we see how MXC is seen in the foreign nation of New Jersey.
Games include: Sack Lunch; Boulder Dash; Tight Enders; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
People with hobbies take on food service employees to see who's got what it takes to survive elimination. Kenny discusses his leisure activities, and the Captain shares his opinion on hobbies with the contestants.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Saddle Sores; Wallbangers; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Hi-Tech vs. Civil Service. Hi-Tech computer nerds take on the Government Civil Servants. It's a bombastic brawl pitting brilliance vs. bureaucracy, MXC style. Games: Dope on a Rope, Circle Jerkers, Swamp Gassers, and Brass Balls.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Cable TV Workers vs. White House Employees
programme.desc:
Finding out who's more ruthless: cable TV workers or White House employees. Meanwhile, Kenny remembers a White House tour.
Games include: Rotating Surfboards of Death; Door Jam; Mine Games; Tumbling Dominoes of Doom

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Lawndale High School goes on another field trip, this time to a local paint-ball course, with Helen & Jake as parental chaperones. The true savage nature of everybody's personality is revealed on this trip. Daria and Jane opt to sneak out toward another nearby tourist attraction that proves to be a complete rip-off.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn needs a quick passing grade in English, and ends up writing an impressive essay(by Lawndale standards, that is), but fears that it might give her a reputation as a nerd. Instead it gives her a new following as a trendy intellectual, something that Daria, a true intellectual can't stand. Since desperate times call for desperate measures, Daria is willing to consider such measures in order to persuade her sister to give up this facade.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and her family participate in a strange and frightening ritual known as a family wedding.

programme.title: Daria
programme.sub-title: That Was Then, This Is Dumb
programme.desc: Helen and Jake's former-hippie friends Willow and Coyote Yeager visit, causing the Morgendorffers to doubt their own value system. Daria and Quinn stick around to use their protest activities against them--and so Quinn can flirt with their son--until Jane invites Daria to help Trent and Jesse sell their parents' old records at a flea market.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When Daria and Jane are assigned to make a student film, they decide to record a day in the life of Quinn.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria gets roped into participating in the Lawndale High yearbook and meets Ted, who up to now has been entirely schooled at home.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Jodie are invited to visit a school for the gifted and talented.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria develops a mysterious rash and has to check into the hospital for tests.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: Danger Mouse on the Orient Express
programme.desc: While on holiday in Venice, DM and Penfold stumble upon a fiendish plot of Greenback's to raise buildings all over Europe and lure all tourists to his museum of Barry Mannilow album covers. Colonel K's office has arranged transport for them aboard the Orient Express, but Greenback has also charted the train for the Euro Master Crooks convention in Paris.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback seeks a truce with Dangermouse to save the Earth from being attacked by an alien army. They agree to meet on Greenback's space station, but Dangermouse and Penfold are unaware they are flying into a trap.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Dr. Frankenstoat has constructed a fiendish device to make electric Vampoids infused with Count Duckula's personality. Danger Mouse infiltrates his castle intend on destroying the machine, but Penfold pushes the start button, releasing a flock of bats.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: Where There's a Well There's a Way
programme.desc: Danger Mouse is sent to find Merlin's magic ink well which will grant the finder a wish.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Top secret blueprints for a world shattering device have been stolen from Puttinghamdown Research station by Mac the Fork. He teams up with Dudley Poyson to build it as Dangermouse and Penfold race to Glasgow to stop them.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: London has been flooded. Indeed, all the sea and land on Earth have swapped places. It appears to be because the moon's gravitational pull has gone haywire, so DM and Penfold head up into outer space.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: London has been shrouded in darkness for almost a week, so Danger Mouse and Penfold head off into outer space to investigate. There they encounter a anti-logic spaceship manned by The Gremlin, who plans to turn the entire Earth Topsy turvy.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Fireproof Handkerchief - Flashed Sign - Fishing Bears - Balancing Glass On Card - Lye & Aluminum - Water Hyacinth - Taste & Smell - Flight Simulator - Breaking Slat

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman demonstrates how people breath using a model of the diaphragm and lungs. Beakman also explains the sending of signals via telephones.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.sub-title: Tape Recordings, Beakmania & Force Vs. Pressure
programme.desc: Beakman demonstrates how cassette players convert sound into electromagnetic signals that get stored onto a tape and delves into the mystery of a bed of nails. Can you really lie on it without getting skewered?

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals how microwave ovens work and shows a cross-section model of a spider that demonstrates how they spin silk. He als discusses how they can be a crucial part of our ecosystem by eating harmful insects.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains the human ear, why we have earwax, and why they pop when they are under pressure. He also introduces Robert Goddard, the father of modern rocketry.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman discusses the ozone layer and why it is rapidly depleting. He also explains the complexities of acids.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Music is the art and science of expressing ideas and feelings through sound. A sad song can say more about how someone feels than most words, and a familiar song can make crowds clap together and feel like one happy family. Whatever the emotion, music seems to have a way to communicate it. The music we listen to today is the result of years of experimentation with sounds. As people figured out what they liked best, they invented instruments that could play their favorite tones and developed popular rhythms, or patterns of beats. Each note of music, and every tone of each instrument is a sound wave. Some sound waves sound great together. Some not so good. Getting the exact soundwaves in the pattern you want – now that’s science.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Things can appear to be moving, when they really aren’t. Sometimes an object might seem to be at rest, even when it is in motion. It’s all relative. Relative motion, that is. How things appear to move depends on how you, or any observer, happens to be moving. So, if you’re on a bus looking out a window into another bus, and your bus begins to back up slowly, you may think the other bus is moving forward, when really it’s not moving at all. That’s motion in motion. It happens all around us all the time.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball grows jealous of Darwin's new talent.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Richard tries to find the kids before Nicole realizes they're gone.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin discover that Rob, who once swore to wreak vengeance on them, has been trying to destroy them for some time… but his traps never work.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Gumball and Darwin decide to join a crew, they are determined to prove their worth to senior citizens.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Anais reveals that she's in the grade above them at school, Gumball and Darwin's eyes are suddenly opened to a whole new world of other students at Elmore Junior High. Gumball is horrified to learn that there are a bunch of characters who are barely aware of his existence and is determined to force his way into their lives.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Richard learns of Granny Jojo's plans to marry, he sets out to adopt Louie so he can forbid the wedding.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball, Darwin and Anais are delighted when Grandpa Louie gives them a check for $5000. They immediately start fantasizing over what to spend it on. But when Mom and Dad say they're going to spend the money on paying bills instead, it's a race to the bank to see who can cash the check first.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When they find Anais training herself for a brutal showdown, Gumball and Darwin realise that their sister is being pestered at school by an annoying kid: Billy. They try to teach her how to resolve the problem peacefully, but Billy has other ideas.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin are crushed to learn that Mr. Robinson is selling his house and moving away, and when trying to make their final moments together last doesn't work, the duo decide to sabotage the sale.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: After they receive an extravagant invitation to Masami's birthday party, Gumball and Darwin are anxious to find just the right gift for her. However, they're not the only ones: it turns out every kid in Elmore is competing to get Masami the perfect present.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: In order to quickly get through their chores and karate lessons, the Titans learn how to montage.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Encoded By RMTeam, http://RMZ.cr

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Not wanting to be creeped out on Easter the Titans dispatch the Easter Bunny and take matters into their own hands.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Batman and Commissioner Gordon go camping but instead of fun outdoor activities they’d rather watch TV.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Thinking it’s a way to reconnect with her father, Raven enters a beauty pageant.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin tries to tell some Greek myths but the other Titans give their own fractured versions instead.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans must find a way to keep all the Jump City residents from moving to Stockton.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin surprises the Titans with a trip to the opera but they soon fall asleep, and all dream about starring in their own operas.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans are at Super Hero Summer Camp for the week and they may need to add another teammate to win the big canoe race.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans give Robin advice to guide him through his camp romance with Wonder Girl.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: After Stimpy gets out of the shower, he finds that there is not a pair of pants in the house. Ignoring Ren, who tells him that clothes are for humans, and that he doesn't need pants, he tries multiple times to find a pair of pants, without much luck. He is shunned by the outside world because he is nude. He eventually gets lost in the forest, cold, naked, and embarrassed.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren's feeling fine on his 10th birthday until Stimpy reminds him that he's 70 years old in "dog years". Ren prepares for death with a will, a fake funeral, and the search for the perfect coffin.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy falls in love with his wonderful belly button and all the fantastic noises it makes. A voice calls him from his belly button and tells him that he can crawl in. Here he meets Jerry the Belly Button Elf, who puts him to work as a domestic servant.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: TV star Haggis MacHaggis (voiced by Alan Young) is kicked out of his mansion and thrown out onto the streets after Ren and Stimpy's cartoon show becomes more popular than his. Just as he plans to end it all, Haggis enlists in the help of two thugs-for-hire who help him sabotage Ren and Stimpy's show.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Desperate to join the Barrette Beret Girls, Ren and Stimpy have to prove themselves to the burly babes.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: In this wildlife documentary, Ren and his stupid assistant observe the natural behavior of the rare animal called the Lummox. Actually an obese, ignorant, dim-witted and unhygienic human, these massive land-beasts exhibit playfulness, curiosity, rage and most intriguing of all, courtship. Which of the two competing ignoramuses underpants has what it takes to win over the female of the species?

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to the hair salon to see the stylist's boobs...and maybe get their hair cut.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: When the boys decide to throw a party so they can 'cop a feel,' it is almost ruined when Stewart and his friends show up, but then Todd arrives and trashes the house.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Buzzcut orders the boys to take a shower after gym class, when suddenly the fire alarm goes off and they are forced to exit the building clad in only their skivvies.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to the animal shelter to get a dog, and find one that really likes Beavis.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Imitating a PBS documentary about Ben Franklin, Beavis and Butt-head get struck by lightning, and attract the interest of ""Betsy Wiener"" from ""Decency In Media.""

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Earthquakes (Kevin & Holly) vs. Chocolate and Vanilla (Tim & Wendy)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Wild, Wild Kingdom will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Lisa gets made-up all flashy looking. When Alasdair insults her new look, she punches him, causing him to think he's different animals.
